name: Asha Vahishta
description: Asha Vahishta, often translated as "Best Righteousness" or "Excellent Truth," is a central concept and divine entity in Zoroastrianism. Asha represents the fundamental cosmic order, the principle of truth, justice, and righteousness that governs the universe. It is not merely a moral code, but an inherent law woven into the fabric of existence, opposing chaos and falsehood (Druj). Asha Vahishta embodies this principle, acting as a divine force that upholds the balance and harmony of creation. Adherence to Asha is paramount for Zoroastrians, dictating ethical conduct and spiritual progress. It is through righteous actions, truthful speech, and pure thoughts that individuals align themselves with Asha and contribute to the cosmic struggle against evil. Asha Vahishta is closely associated with fire, a symbol of purity and divine presence, and is often invoked in prayers and rituals. The concept of Asha is foundational to Zoroastrian eschatology, influencing beliefs about the afterlife and the ultimate triumph of good over evil. The Amesha Spenta, the Holy Immortals, are emanations of Ahura Mazda, and Asha Vahishta is considered one of the most important, embodying divine law and order.
subtitle: Best Righteousness

keyMyths.title: The Invocation of Asha
keyMyths.description: In Zoroastrian prayers and rituals, Asha Vahishta is frequently invoked to purify the environment and strengthen the forces of good. The act of invoking Asha involves reciting sacred verses from the Avesta, particularly the Gathas, which are believed to embody the essence of truth and righteousness. Through these invocations, practitioners seek to align themselves with the cosmic order, ward off evil influences, and promote harmony in the world. This highlights the active role individuals play in maintaining Asha.
keyMyths.source: Boyce, Mary. *Zoroastrians: Their Religious Beliefs and Practices.* Routledge, 2001.

keyMyths.title: Asha and the Judgment of Souls
keyMyths.description: According to Zoroastrian eschatology, after death, the soul undergoes a period of judgment. The soul's deeds, words, and thoughts are weighed on a cosmic scale, with Asha serving as the standard of righteousness. Those who have lived in accordance with Asha are rewarded with paradise, while those who have strayed from the path of truth face punishment. This belief underscores the importance of living a virtuous life guided by Asha's principles, as it directly impacts one's eternal destiny. This judgement is overseen by Mithra, Rashnu, and Sraosha.
keyMyths.source: Dhalla, Maneckji Nusservanji. *Zoroastrian Theology.* AMS Press, 1972.

keyMyths.title: Asha's Role in the Frashokereti
keyMyths.description: The Frashokereti, or Renovation of the World, is a central concept in Zoroastrianism representing the ultimate triumph of good over evil. Asha Vahishta plays a crucial role in this cosmic event, as the restoration of the world to its original state of perfection is achieved through the complete eradication of falsehood and the full establishment of truth and righteousness. The Frashokereti signifies the final victory of Asha over Druj, ushering in an era of peace, harmony, and eternal bliss for all righteous beings.
keyMyths.source: Hintze, Almut. *A Zoroastrian Treasury of Wisdom.* Oxford University Press, 2018.
